title: unona altissima
intro: Unona altissima, also known as the yellow ginger or wild ginger, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Zingiberaceae family, commonly found in the tropical forests of West Africa. The medicinal properties of Unona altissima include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and ointments, as well as being used in traditional cooking and medicine.
